ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product; this item is a double-patty variant with mushroom and creamy black pepper mayo sauce. No exact FoodOn term exists for this specific commercial variant; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Hamburger) is the closest defensible code. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a double-patty mushroom burger variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps specifically to this commercial burger variant. SNOMED CT food coding is not applicable here.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one whole burger weighing around 230 g, comprising two beef patties, a bun, grilled mushrooms, and creamy black pepper mayo sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables (SR Legacy 21110); supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for double beef burger with sauce
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a creamy mushroom double beef burger as served by McDonald's Singapore. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy item 21110 (Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables, fast food) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable double beef burgers with sauce. Mushroom and creamy mayo sauce components add modest fat and sodium. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this class of fast-food double beef burger. Serving size estimated at 230 g based on comparable McDonald's double-patty products.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun / fast-food burger GI estimate based on white wheat bread and mixed fast-food burger analogues; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore creamy mushroom double beef burger. GI estimated at 66 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for hamburger / fast-food burger (GI ~66, medium category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values. The wheat bun is the primary glycemic driver; beef patties and sauce contribute negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 50.6) / 100 ≈ 33.4, rounded to 33.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1104 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger as a single serving provides approximately 46–55% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg, which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 66) combined with high glycemic load per serving (~33) and refined carbohydrate from wheat bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger only occasionally and in controlled portions, as the high glycemic load per serving (~33) from the refined wheat bun can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and avoiding sugary beverages is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~5.4 g per 100 g; ~12.4 g per serving), trans fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its significant saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and elevated cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Double Beef Burger (marketed as 'Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe') is a fast-food burger served at McDonald's Singapore outlets. It consists of two grilled beef patties stacked in a soft wheat bun, topped with perfectly seasoned grilled mushrooms and a luxurious creamy black pepper mayonnaise sauce. The burger is listed as Halal-certified on the field survey data platform. Texturally, it delivers a juicy, savoury beef flavour complemented by the earthy umami of grilled mushrooms and the rich, peppery creaminess of the mayo sauce. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense, high-protein, high-fat, and high-sodium fast-food item. Per 100 g it provides approximately 265 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 14.2 g fat (of which 5.4 g is saturated), and 22 g carbohydrate. A full serving (~230 g) delivers roughly 610 kcal and over 1100 mg sodium. Its medium glycemic index (~66) and high glycemic load (~33 per serving) make it a significant concern for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The elevated saturated fat and sodium content also raise concerns for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular meal, and individuals managing chronic conditions should exercise caution. The dish is classified as ultra-processed fast food within the beef burger / fast-food sandwich subgroup.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe halal?
What is in the creamy black pepper mayo sauce?
How much sodium is in a McDonald's double mushroom burger?
Is this burger suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of a double beef burger?
Can I eat a double beef burger if I have high blood pressure?
How much protein is in a double patty mushroom burger?
What allergens are in the Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe?
Is the Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe available in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore menu item in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded). Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy item 21110 (fast-food double beef burger with condiments and vegetables), which is the closest defensible analogue. The GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous fast-food burger data. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 21110 (Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables, fast food) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable double beef burgers. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry was found for this item. 2. Serving size of 230 g is estimated based on comparable McDonald's double-patty burger weights; actual weight may vary by outlet. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database fast-food burger data (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 5. The input food_group 'Vegetables' has been corrected to 'Meat and alternatives' as this is a beef-dominant burger product. 6. Halal status is preserved from the field survey data cuisine_tags input. 7. Trans fat (~0.4 g per 100 g) is estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for comparable fast-food double beef burgers and may vary by beef supplier.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record. Identified that food_group 'Vegetables' from input was incorrect; corrected to 'Meat and alternatives'. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Confirmed GI source is University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed journal. Confirmed no zero core macros requiring justification.
